projectDescription: A community-driven C++ reimplementation of Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas (1.0 US) that replaces original game functions with reversed and documented equivalents via an injected DLL.
keyFeatures: Function-level reimplementation of GTA:SA injected as a DLL via ASI Loader
Reversible hook system allowing toggling between original and reversed function implementations
Reversible bug fix framework with named, creditable fixes that can be enabled/disabled
Modern C++23 codebase with ~7965 reversed functions across 700 classes (~79% complete)
In-game debug tools menu (ImGui-based) for testing and debugging reversed functions
SDL3 + OpenAL audio backend with full GTA:SA audio engine reimplementation
MTA:SA (Multi Theft Auto) compatibility and plugin-sdk integration
Complete entity hierarchy: Ped, Vehicle (Automobile, Bike, Boat, Heli, Plane, Train), Building, Object
publicApiSurface: ReversibleHooks::Install — installs a function hook redirecting GTA calls to reversed implementation
ReversibleHooks::InstallVirtual — installs a virtual function table hook
notsa::bugfixes::* — named reversible bug fix definitions (GenericCrashing, GenericOOB, etc.)
notsa::IsFixBugs() — runtime check whether bug fixes are enabled
RH_ScopedClass / RH_ScopedInstall macros — DSL for registering hooks in InjectHooks()
CPed, CPlayerPed, CVehicle, CAutomobile, CBike, CBoat, CHeli, CPlane — core entity classes
CWorld, CStreaming, CPathFind, CRadar, CCamera — major game subsystem managers
TheScripts / CRunningScript — GTA:SA script (SCM/Opcode) execution engine
CAudioEngine, CAESound, CAEVehicleAudioEntity — audio subsystem classes
